title: arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. crassifolius
intro: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. crassifolius, also known as the bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other issues related to the urinary system. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin, a compound that has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal effects, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. crassifolius is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to support kidney health and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections.
